pi_items.source: task.json description, setup_task.sh DROP statement, export_result.sh query, and verifier.py checks confirm this is the required view name.
task.json description, setup_task.sh rm statement, export_result.sh, and verifier.py confirm this is the required output CSV file.
setup_task.sh inserts flights with IDs 100, 200, 800, 900 into the FLIGHT_LOGS table. export_result.sh queries for these specific IDs.
setup_task.sh and verifier.py document Flight 900 (JFK->LHR on Mar 15, 2024) as the 'gap week' edge case.
setup_task.sh and verifier.py document Flight 100 (HND->SFO on Jan 1, 2024) as the Date Line Crossing edge case.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses synthetic flight data to test timezone handling. It requires creating a view named V_FLIGHT_ANALYSIS and exporting a CSV to /home/ga/Desktop/long_haul_report.csv. The data includes specific edge cases: Flight 100 crosses the International Date Line (HND to SFO), and Flight 900 occurs during a 'gap week' (Mar 15, 2024) where US DST has started but UK DST has not, testing dynamic timezone offsets instead of fixed ones. The test flights inserted and verified are 100, 200, 800, and 900.
